title: Flooded City
description: Something terrible has happened and the city is in ruins. When trying to gather your thoughts it seems you aren't too safe yet as the water levels are rising.
body:
# Flooded City by Forge Labs ![Forge Labs Divider](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/f83f327701a64468e9152ecf2450c0ef1be54a3f.png)
Something terrible has happened and the city is in ruins. When trying to gather your thoughts it seems you aren't too safe yet as the water levels are rising. The water is full of terrific creatures that can tear you apart. What will you do to survive the rising water and the creatures within?
![Forge Labs Divider](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/f83f327701a64468e9152ecf2450c0ef1be54a3f.png)
![Forge Labs Divider](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/f83f327701a64468e9152ecf2450c0ef1be54a3f.png)
### Notice! To add the rising water levels you will for now have to use Worldedit. Please note that this is a very demanding process. This process replaces millions of blocks at a time. It is not recommended for low end hardware. - Type in the following in chat **//pos1 1174,62,903**. This will set the first position to be one of the corners of the city. - Then, type **//pos2 -228,x,-70** where **x** represents the amount you want to raise the water level by. **For example if you wanted to raise the water level by 10, you'd replace x with 72.** This is because we have added 10 to the current water level value, which is 62. - Finally, in chat you'll want to enter the command **//replace 0 water**. It will take some time depending on your PC, but you should notice an increase in the water level. - When raising the water level again, you'll want to adjust the y value in both position commands to ensure that not you're covering blocks you've already filled. For pos1, set the y value to be the y value of pos2, which would be 72. For pos2, you'll want to set the y value to be this new y value plus however many blocks you want to raise the water level by. If we wanted to do 10 again, we'd do 82. #### And that's it! Repeat this process every 10 days if you want the water level to increase regularly.
### 🔬 World used [Open World Zombie Apocalypse!](https://www.planetminecraft.com/project/open-world-zombie-apocalypse-survival)

### ⚗️ Shader Packs [Complementary Shaders - Unbound](https://modrinth.com/shader/complementary-unbound)
![Forge Labs Divider](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/f83f327701a64468e9152ecf2450c0ef1be54a3f.png)
中文翻译 (Chinese Translation) # 洪水淹没的城市》由 Forge Labs 制作 ![Forge Labs Divider](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/f83f327701a64468e9152ecf2450c0ef1be54a3f.png)
可怕的事情发生了，城市变成了一片废墟。当你试图整理思绪时，水位正在上升，看来你还不太安全。水中充满了可怕的生物，它们会把你撕成碎片。你将如何在上涨的水位和水中的生物中生存下来？
![Forge Labs Divider](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/f83f327701a64468e9152ecf2450c0ef1be54a3f.png)
![Forge Labs Divider](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/f83f327701a64468e9152ecf2450c0ef1be54a3f.png)
### 注意！ 要添加上升的水位，您现在必须使用 世界编辑器 (Worldedit)。 请注意，这是一个要求很高的过程。这个过程会一次替换数百万个区块。不建议使用低端硬件。 - 在聊天中键入以下内容 **//pos1 1174,62,903**。这将把第一个位置设置为城市的一个角落。 - 然后，输入 **//pos2 -228,x,-70**，其中 **x** 代表你想提高的水位。**例如，如果你想将水位提高 10，就需要将 x 替换为 72。**这是因为我们在当前水位值 62 的基础上增加了 10。 - 最后，在聊天中输入命令 **//replace 0 water**。这需要一些时间，具体取决于你的电脑，但你应该会发现水位有所上升。 - 再次提高水位时，你需要调整两个位置命令中的 y 值，以确保不会覆盖已经填满的区块。对于位置 1，将 y 值设置为位置 2 的 y 值，即 72。对于位置 2，你需要将 y 值设置为这个新的 y 值加上你想要提高水位的区块数。如果我们想再做 10 次，那么就做 82 次。 #### 就这样！如果想让水位定期上升，每 10 天重复一次这个过程。
